summary: The active branch is phase/macos-protocol-hosts-and-local-shell. Hosts now carry explicit protocol metadata, the native Tauri bridge can launch a real macOS login shell session, and SSH-only features stay hidden or disabled for non-SSH entries. The release-secret blocker remains open but is no longer the active execution slice.
next_steps: Implement executable telnet, serial, and mosh transports on top of the new protocol-aware host model.
Add solo-macOS daily-driver features that build on the local shell path, starting with session history/logging and richer runbook execution.
Preserve the blocked release-secret provisioning work until exportable signing and notarization material exists.
blockers: GitHub release secret provisioning is still blocked because the repository has no configured signing/notarization secrets and the current workstation state does not expose exportable certificate or notary credential material.
Telnet, serial, and mosh are modeled in inventory but do not yet have executable native transports.
